intro: Adjust carries low lock-in (Attribution history in proprietary schema; painful to migrate MMPs) while AppsFlyer is medium (Medium — attribution data tied to to AppsFlyer ecosystem). Both tools position themselves for mobile, so the decision rarely comes down to surface features. In practice, Adjust — Mobile measurement and analytics platform with deterministic and probabilistic attribution, fraud prevention, and audience segmentation. AppsFlyer — Mobile attribution and marketing analytics platform with deterministic matching, deep links, and Privacy Sandbox support. Adjust runs on custom; AppsFlyer runs on usage from $0.05-0.07/install (Growth). AppsFlyer rates 3/5 on transparency versus 1/5 for Adjust. Both tools list mobile apps with paid acquisition as a headline strength, which is why pricing and lock-in usually decide the call. Both tools surface overlapping when-to-use scenarios, including mobile apps with paid acquisition. The honest trade-off: Adjust is not built for web-only products, and AppsFlyer is not the right call for web-only products, so neither is a universal default.
quick_take: Adjust is for mobile; AppsFlyer overlaps on the same job; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Adjust if mobile and you want low switching cost.
verdict_b: Choose AppsFlyer if mobile and the $0.05-0.07/install (Growth) entry price fits your budget.

faq.a: The listed pricing for Adjust is custom and for AppsFlyer is usage from $0.05-0.07/install (Growth), which doesn't give a clean apples-to-apples cost. Unit economics and plan structure differ enough that only your real volume answers this. Price both against the same workload.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Adjust to AppsFlyer?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving Adjust is relatively light thanks to its low lock-in, and onboarding to AppsFlyer involves medium lock-in (Medium — attribution data tied to to AppsFlyer ecosystem). Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Adjust or AppsFlyer?
faq.a: On the 0-5 developer experience score in our data, AppsFlyer rates 5/5 while Adjust rates 4/5, giving AppsFlyer the edge. That score is a rough summary; actual day-to-day DX depends on your language, deployment target, and tolerance for edge cases. Prototype both to validate.

faq.q: Is AppsFlyer a good alternative to Adjust?
faq.a: Yes, AppsFlyer targets the same job Adjust does — mobile — so functionally it slots in as an alternative. Pricing model (usage from $0.05-0.07/install (Growth) vs custom) and lock-in (medium lock-in (Medium — attribution data tied to to AppsFlyer ecosystem) vs low lock-in (Attribution history in proprietary schema; painful to migrate MMPs)) usually decide which is the better fit. Validate on your actual workload. Note: AppsFlyer is explicitly not for web-only products.
